agent_raw:
The leadership readout does not justify approving the policy change. Four problems undermine the benchmark.

The most fundamental issue is objective misalignment. The incumbent was built to maximize 90-day renewal probability. The challenger is evaluated on net contribution margin per targeted subscriber. These are different objectives. A policy that performs well on margin may perform differently on renewal probability, and vice versa. The comparison mixes policies optimized for different goals.

The second issue is holdout contamination in the doubly robust evaluation. The DR nuisance models — both outcome and propensity — were trained on all four deployment quarters, including Q3-Q4. Q3-Q4 is also the reporting window. The evaluator's estimates are not independent of the reporting data.

The third issue is threshold selection on the reporting slice. The operating cutoff was selected to maximize margin on Q3-Q4, and headline results are reported on Q3-Q4. Selecting and evaluating on the same slice inflates the apparent margin per subscriber.

The fourth issue is asymmetric calibration: three rounds of Bayesian hyperparameter search for the challenger versus 18-month-old original calibration for the incumbent. This is not a symmetric comparison.

The correct path is to refit nuisance models on earlier quarters only, select the threshold on a pre-reporting period, update the incumbent's calibration to the same margin objective, and evaluate both policies on the same frozen holdout.
all_issues_raised: Objective changed between incumbent (renewal probability) and challenger (net contribution margin)
DR nuisance models fit on full experiment log including Q3-Q4 reporting period
Operating cutoff selected on same Q3-Q4 cohort used for headline reporting
Asymmetric optimization: challenger received Bayesian tuning, incumbent on 18-month-old calibration

empirical_test.condition: Refit nuisance models excluding reporting slice, select cutoff on pre-reporting validation set, re-calibrate incumbent to same margin objective, evaluate both on frozen Q3-Q4 holdout
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Challenger profit lift falls below zero or CI includes zero under clean holdout protocol on shared metric
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Challenger beats re-optimized incumbent by at least 3% incremental profit with block-aware 95% CI excluding zero
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Positive but sub-3% lift, or heterogeneous gains across regions with some negative
